# 🤖 AI-Assisted Test Case Generation & Review

**Task:** Used Claude AI to generate API test cases for JSONPlaceholder /users endpoint  
**Tester/Reviewer:** Mahaya Zeb Khan  
**Date:** 08 March 2026  
**Purpose:** Demonstrate AI-assisted QA workflow with human review and validation  

---

## PART 1 — AI Generated Test Cases (Raw Output)

*The following test cases were generated by Claude AI (claude.ai)*

### AI Prompt Used:
> "Generate comprehensive API test cases for a REST API endpoint
> GET /users that returns a list of users"

### AI Output:

| TC ID | Test Scenario | Method | Input | Expected Result |
|-------|--------------|--------|-------|-----------------|
| AI-TC-001 | Get all users successfully | GET | /users | 200 OK, array of users |
| AI-TC-002 | Verify response time is acceptable | GET | /users | Response < 2000ms |
| AI-TC-003 | Verify response is valid JSON | GET | /users | Content-Type: application/json |
| AI-TC-004 | Verify each user has required fields | GET | /users | id, name, email present in each user |
| AI-TC-005 | Get user with valid ID | GET | /users/1 | 200 OK, single user object |
| AI-TC-006 | Get user with invalid ID | GET | /users/999 | 404 Not Found |
| AI-TC-007 | Get user with string ID | GET | /users/abc | 400 Bad Request |
| AI-TC-008 | Get user with negative ID | GET | /users/-1 | 400 Bad Request |
| AI-TC-009 | Get user with ID zero | GET | /users/0 | 404 Not Found |
| AI-TC-010 | Verify pagination works | GET | /users?page=2 | 200 OK, different set of users |

---

## PART 2 — My Human Review & Validation

*This section shows my QA judgement applied to AI output*

### ✅ Good Suggestions by AI:
- **AI-TC-002** — Response time check is excellent. I had not included this in my manual tests. Will add to Postman assertions.
- **AI-TC-003** — Checking Content-Type header is a best practice I will now include in all API tests.
- **AI-TC-004** — Field-level validation is critical. AI correctly identified we should verify each field exists.
- **AI-TC-007 & AI-TC-008** — Negative tests with string and negative IDs are edge cases I had missed. Very valuable.

### ⚠️ AI Suggestions I Questioned & Tested:

| TC ID | AI Said | I Tested | Actual Result | My Verdict |
|-------|---------|----------|---------------|------------|
| AI-TC-007 | GET /users/abc → 400 Bad Request | Tested in Postman | Returns 404 Not Found | ❌ AI wrong — predicted 400, actual 404 |
| AI-TC-008 | GET /users/-1 → 400 Bad Request | Tested in Postman | Returns 404 Not Found | ❌ AI wrong — predicted 400, actual 404 |
| AI-TC-009 | GET /users/0 → 404 Not Found | Tested in Postman | Returns 404 Not Found | ✅ AI was correct |

### 🔍 My Additional Observations:
1. AI did not suggest testing **SQL injection** in URL params — I would add: GET /users/1' as a security edge case
2. AI did not suggest testing with **very large ID numbers** — GET /users/999999999
3. AI assumed standard REST behaviour — always verify assumptions against the ACTUAL API

### 📊 AI Output Quality Assessment:

| Category | Score | Notes |
|----------|-------|-------|
| Coverage | 8/10 | Good variety of scenarios |
| Accuracy | 7/10 | 2 out of 10 predictions were wrong |
| Edge Cases | 6/10 | Missed security and extreme value tests |
| Overall | 7/10 | Good starting point, needs human validation |

---

## PART 3 — Key Learning

> AI tools are powerful for **generating ideas and coverage quickly**
> but they cannot replace a QA engineer's judgement.
> AI predicted wrong behaviour for 2 test cases — without human
> review and actual testing, those errors would have gone unnoticed.
> 
> The best QA workflow = AI for speed + Human for accuracy.

---

## ✅ Final Test Cases Added to Suite After Review

Based on AI suggestions + my validation, I added these NEW test cases
to my main test suite:
- TC-005: Verify response time under 2000ms
- TC-006: Verify Content-Type is application/json  
- TC-007: GET /users/abc — string ID (actual result: 200 empty)
- TC-008: GET /users/-1 — negative ID (actual result: 200 empty)
- TC-009: GET /users/0 — zero ID (actual result: 404)
```
